original_hypothesis.title: BlackClaims recoverERC20 Can Drain Active Season Reward Tokens
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The recoverERC20() function allows the owner to withdraw ANY ERC20 token, including the reward token (BLACK), from the contract. During an active claiming season, calling recoverERC20(address(token)) would drain the contract's reward balance, causing subsequent claimAndStakeReward calls to fail due to insufficient balance, without updating remaining_reward_amount.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Unlike emergencyRecoverERC20 in Bribe.sol which is documented as dangerous, BlackClaims.recoverERC20 has no exclusion of the reward token and no accounting adjustment. The contract's dev comment suggests it's for recovering accidentally sent tokens, but it can drain active season funds.

proof_of_signal.pattern: No check that tokenAddress_ != address(token); transfers full balance to msg.sender

preconditions: Owner calls recoverERC20 with the reward token address

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: No exclusion of reward token from recovery; transfers full balance without accounting update
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: remaining_reward_amount not updated, causing _preClaim to succeed but token.transfer to fail
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Missing require(tokenAddress_ != address(token)) or accounting adjustment

analysis_questions: Is this intentional as an emergency mechanism?
Does the owner role have other mechanisms to drain funds?
Can remaining_reward_amount underflow after partial recovery?
validation_requirements: Test recoverERC20 with reward token during active season
Check if revokeUnclaimedReward is the intended recovery path

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Compromised or malicious owner calls recoverERC20(address(token)) during active claiming season, draining all BLACK tokens. Users who haven't claimed yet find claimAndStakeReward reverts on token transfer, permanently losing their seasonal rewards.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Loss of all unclaimed seasonal rewards
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users with unclaimed season rewards
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Owner could return tokens, but no on-chain enforcement

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for recoverERC20 with reward token during active season

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
